serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Jollibee Spicy Spaghetti Platter serving is approximately 380 g, comprising a portion of cooked spaghetti topped with spicy meat sauce and a cheese garnish, served as a complete meal.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with meat sauce, fast food type — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; adapted for spicy variant with reference to Jollibee published nutrition panel
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Spaghetti with meat sauce' (FDC ID 173949) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values adapted to reflect the spicy variant served at Jollibee Singapore, which includes a chilli-enriched tomato meat sauce and a cheese topping. Serving size of 380 g is consistent with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information for the Spaghetti platter. Per-100g values are derived from the USDA base with minor upward adjustment to sodium and fat to reflect the fast food preparation context.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — spaghetti, white, boiled
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 45 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white spaghetti, boiled (GI range 38–52, representative value 45). The spicy meat sauce and cheese topping do not substantially alter the GI of the pasta base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 67.6) / 100 ≈ 30.4, where carbohydrate per 380 g serving = 17.8 × 3.8 = 67.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from spicy meat sauce and fast food preparation
hypertension.guidance: A single 380 g serving provides approximately 1178 mg sodium (≈51% of the 2300 mg daily limit), making this dish a high-sodium concern for individuals managing hypertension; portion reduction or sauce modification is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined pasta carbohydrates with added sugars in the sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: The glycemic load per serving is approximately 30, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a platter and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, sodium, and refined carbohydrate content from fast food preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~6 g per serving), high sodium (~1178 mg per serving), and refined carbohydrates makes this dish moderately concerning for cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption within a heart-healthy dietary pattern is acceptable but regular intake is not recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Spaghetti Platter is a fast food pasta dish served at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet. It consists of a generous portion of cooked white spaghetti topped with a sweet-spicy tomato-based meat sauce made from ground beef or pork, chilli, onion, garlic, and a blend of seasonings, finished with a cheese garnish. Jollibee's spaghetti is a signature Filipino-style pasta dish that is characteristically sweeter and spicier than Italian-style spaghetti bolognese, reflecting Filipino culinary preferences. The texture is soft and saucy, with a mildly spicy and savoury-sweet flavour profile. The platter is served as a complete meal and is certified Halal at the Singapore outlet. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate source of protein and carbohydrates, with notable sodium content from the meat sauce. The glycemic index of the pasta base is low to medium (GI ≈ 45), but the glycemic load per full serving is high due to the large carbohydrate portion. Clinically, the dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its high sodium content, and for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic load per serving. The saturated fat and sodium content also make it moderately relevant for cardiovascular disease management. It is suitable for Halal dietary requirements but contains gluten, dairy, and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: What is the calorie count of Jollibee Spicy Spaghetti?
Is Jollibee spaghetti Halal in Singapore?
How much sodium is in Jollibee Spicy Spaghetti Platter?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for Jollibee Spicy Spaghetti Platter Singapore exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Spaghetti with meat sauce' with adjustments for the fast food spicy variant context. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using white spaghetti as the analogous food. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy source for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy source for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy source for this food category. Serving size of 380 g is based on Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information for the Spaghetti platter, which is the closest available reference for the Singapore outlet. The Halal flag is set to true based on the cuisine_tags in the input record indicating Halal certification at Jollibee Bugis Village Singapore. The spicy variant of Jollibee spaghetti is a Filipino-style sweet-spicy pasta dish distinct from Italian bolognese. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white spaghetti entry (GI ≈ 45); gi_category is low per the <55 threshold convention.
